content.text:
<user_query>
In C:/Users/andre/.cursor/worktrees/tpn/cse, find existing tests for calibration enrichment. Propose exact backend tests to validate deterministic deep-scan requirements: all keys present, unknown markers, sources mapping, ai_errors logging, contact extraction, multipage crawl. Return target test files and test case skeletons aligned to existing pytest patterns.
</user_query>
Searching the codebase for calibration enrichment and deep-scan tests and reviewing pytest patterns.

Here is a concise map of what already exists, what the handler actually returns, and concrete pytest skeletons you can drop in. ## Existing tests touching calibration / website enrichment | Location | What it covers | |----------|----------------| | [`backend/tests/test_iteration127_trinity_deep_scan.py`](backend/tests/test_iteration127_trinity_deep_scan.py) | `POST /api/enrichment/website` without auth (401/403/422); static checks that `/enrichment/website` exists on the calibration router and that `force_trinity` / `onboarding_deep_scan` appear in `calibration.py`. | | [`backend/tests/test_iteration_130_production.py`](backend/tests/test_iteration_130_production.py) — `TestCalibrationDeepScan.test_08_calibration_enrichment_deep_scan` | Live `requests` call with auth; scores 7 “deep” fields; soft assert `score >= 2`; bails on `status == "error"`. | | [`backend/tests/test_ai_modules_iteration129.py`](backend/tests/test_ai_modules_iteration129.py) — `test_08_calibration_enrichment_quality` | Same live pattern; prints `sources` keys and quality score; **no hard asserts** on full schema. | | [`backend/tests/test_iteration27_phase2_deconstruction.py`](backend/tests/test_iteration27_phase2_deconstruction.py) | Unauthenticated `POST /api/enrichment/website` → 401/403. | There are **no** current tests that lock a **deterministic JSON contract** (full key set, `sources` shape, crawl page list, or AI failure surfacing). Integration tests also depend on Serper, httpx fetches, and edge functions, so they are inherently non-deterministic unless heavily mocked. ## Implementation anchors (for what “deterministic” can mean today) - Scan builds a large `enrichment` dict and returns `{"status": "draft", "url", "enrichment", "message"}` — see [`backend/routes/calibration.py`](backend/routes/calibration.py) around the `enrichment = { ... }` block and `return` at ~1326–1331. - `sources` includes `company`, `competitors`, `abn`, `reviews`, `social_search`, `crawled_pages`, and later `edge_tools` (~1083–1095, ~1300–1322). - Multipage behavior is `_extract_internal_links` + `_crawl_site_context` (~84–148, used at ~996–998). - **`ai_errors` is not a field** in the API response today; failures go to `logger.warning` (~1053–1054). Any test named `ai_errors` either asserts **logging** (e.g. `caplog`) or assumes you **add** a structured field in the handler. - **Contact extraction** (phone/email from HTML) is **not** implemented in the scan path beyond skipping `mailto:` / `tel:` links in `_extract_internal_links`. Tests for “contact extraction” are **spec / TDD** unless you add extractors. --- ## Where to put new tests 1. **`backend/tests/test_calibration_enrichment_deterministic.py`** (new) — **unit / contract** tests: mock `get_ai_response`, `serper_search`, `httpx`, `_call_edge_function`, and optionally use `TestClient` like [`test_profile_autofill.py`](backend/tests/test_profile_autofill.py). This is the right place for “all keys”, `sources`, crawl structure, and (with mocks) AI failure behavior. 2. **`backend/tests/test_iteration127_trinity_deep_scan.py`** (optional extension) — add a **pure function** class `TestCrawlAndLinks` that imports `_extract_internal_links` / `_crawl_site_context` from `routes.calibration` (same style as existing `TestCalibrationEnrichmentCode` that reads/opens backend files). Keeps crawl tests next to iteration-127 “deep scan” theme. 3. **Live integration** (optional): extend [`test_iteration_130_production.py`](backend/tests/test_iteration_130_production.py) or [`test_ai_modules_iteration129.py`](backend/tests/test_ai_modules_iteration129.py) only for smoke; **do not** use them for strict determinism. Below, skeletons follow **pytest**, **`@pytest.mark.asyncio`** for async helpers, **`unittest.mock.patch`**, and **`TestClient` from `fastapi.testclient`** where useful. --- ## Skeleton: `test_calibration_enrichment_deterministic.py` ```python """ Deterministic contract tests for POST /api/calibration enrichment scan. Mock external I/O (Serper, httpx fetch, edge functions, optional get_ai_response). """ import pytest from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch, MagicMock from fastapi.testclient import TestClient from server import app # same as test_profile_autofill # Canonical keys expected on every successful draft scan (adjust to your product contract). REQUIRED_ENRICHMENT_KEYS = frozenset({ "title", "description", "business_name", "industry", "main_products_services", "target_market", "unique_value_proposition", "competitive_advantages", "competitors", "competitor_analysis", "market_position", "abn", "abn_candidates", "social_handles", "trust_signals", "executive_summary", "confidence", "cmo_executive_brief", "seo_analysis", "paid_media_analysis", "social_media_analysis", "website_health", "swot", "competitor_swot", "cmo_priority_actions", "sources", "analysis_gaps", }) REQUIRED_SOURCES_KEYS = frozenset({ "company", "competitors", "abn", "reviews", "social_search", "crawled_pages", }) @pytest.fixture def client(): return TestClient(app) @pytest.fixture def auth_headers(): # Prefer same pattern as test_profile_autofill._register, or patch get_current_user_from_request. pytest.skip("Provide real Bearer token or patch auth dependency") # return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"} class TestEnrichmentDraftContract: """All keys present + sources mapping shape (mocked backend).""" @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_scan_enrichment_has_all_required_keys(self, client, auth_headers): """After mocks, assert REQUIRED_ENRICHMENT_KEYS <= enrichment.keys().""" pytest.skip("Implement patches for serper_search, _fetch_html_and_text, _crawl_site_context, _call_edge_function, get_ai_response") # res = client.post("/api/enrichment/website", headers=auth_headers, json={"url": "https://example.test", "action": "scan"}) # assert res.status_code == 200 # body = res.json() # assert body.get("status") == "draft" # enr = body.get("enrichment") or {} # missing = REQUIRED_ENRICHMENT_KEYS - enr.keys() # assert not missing, f"Missing keys: {missing}" def test_sources_subkeys_and_types(self, client, auth_headers): """sources.company/competitors/abn/reviews are lists; social_search is dict with platform keys; crawled_pages is list of {url, text_len}.""" pytest.skip("Same mock stack as above") # enr = ... # src = enr.get("sources") or {} # assert REQUIRED_SOURCES_KEYS <= src.keys() # assert isinstance(src["crawled_pages"], list) # for p in src["crawled_pages"]: # assert "url" in p and "text_len" in p class TestUnknownMarkers: """Unknown / low-signal markers in deterministic builders (not raw LLM text).""" def test_paid_media_maturity_unknown_when_no_ads_signals(self): """Call _build_paid_media_analysis with corpus lacking paid keywords; expect maturity 'unknown_or_low_visibility' or equivalent.""" pytest.skip("Import _build_paid_media_analysis from routes.calibration; pass minimal text") # from routes.calibration import _build_paid_media_analysis # out = _build_paid_media_analysis("we help local businesses with strategy") # assert out.get("maturity") == "unknown_or_low_visibility" def test_ai_json_empty_strings_not_literal_unknown(self): """If mocking get_ai_response to return JSON with 'unknown' strings, optional guard: strip or normalize (document expected behavior).""" pytest.skip("Only if product forbids literal 'unknown' in user-facing fields") class TestAiErrorsLogging: """Today: log on AI failure; optional future: ai_errors array on enrichment.""" @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_get_ai_response_failure_logs_warning(self, caplog): """Patch get_ai_response to raise; run scan; assert caplog.records / 'AI synthesis unavailable'.""" pytest.skip("Use caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING) and patch routes.calibration.get_ai_response") # import logging # with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING): # ... trigger website_enrichment scan ... # assert any("AI synthesis unavailable" in r.message for r in caplog.records) def test_optional_ai_errors_field_when_implemented(self, client, auth_headers): """If you add enrichment['ai_errors'], assert list of {stage, detail} on failure.""" pytest.skip("Un-skip when product adds ai_errors to JSON") class TestContactExtraction: """Spec tests until _extract_contact_from_html (or similar) exists on scan path.""" def test_contact_page_included_in_internal_links(self): """HTML with should be eligible in _extract_internal_links.""" pytest.skip("Import _extract_internal_links; pass base_url + html fixture") def test_phone_and_email_extracted_from_fixture_html(self): """When implement: assert phone/email keys on enrichment or dedicated contact_hints dict.""" pytest.skip("TDD — no contact extraction in calibration scan today") class TestMultipageCrawl: """Deterministic crawl behavior without real network.""" @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_crawl_respects_max_pages(self): """Patch _fetch_html_and_text to return text; seed_html with N internal links; assert len(pages) <= max_pages.""" pytest.skip("from routes.calibration import _crawl_site_context; patch _fetch_html_and_text") @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_external_and_mailto_links_excluded(self): """_extract_internal_links ignores mailto:, tel:, off-domain hrefs.""" pytest.skip("Import _extract_internal_links; assert returned list only in-domain paths") ``` --- ## Skeleton: optional additions in `test_iteration127_trinity_deep_scan.py` Aligned with existing **`TestCalibrationEnrichmentCode`** style (import from `routes.calibration`, no `TestClient`): ```python class TestDeepScanCrawlDeterminism: """Iteration 127 — multipage crawl helpers (pure, mocked I/O).""" @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_extract_internal_links_prioritizes_intel_paths(self): pytest.skip("from routes.calibration import _extract_internal_links") # html = '''...x''' # links = _extract_internal_links(html, "https://corp.example", limit=8) # assert any("/about" in u for u in links) # assert not any("/random" in u for u in links) # if /random not in allowed path tokens @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_crawl_site_context_aggregates_text(self): pytest.skip("mock _fetch_html_and_text inside routes.calibration") ``` --- ## Notes so expectations stay honest - **Strict “all keys present”** belongs in **mocked** `TestClient` tests; live tests against `thestrategysquad.com.au` will flake when Serper/edge/AI change. - **`ai_errors`**: grep shows **no** symbol in `backend/`; use **`caplog`** until the API exposes errors. - **Contact extraction**: not in the current scan implementation; keep tests as **`pytest.skip`** TDD or implement extraction first. - **Auth**: either reuse `_register` from [`test_profile_autofill.py`](backend/tests/test_profile_autofill.py) or `patch("routes.calibration.get_current_user_from_request", new=AsyncMock(return_value={"id": "test-user", "email": "t@t.com"}))` so tests do not hit Supabase. If you want these turned into committed tests, switch to Agent mode and specify whether auth should be patched or real tokens.
